title: Senior/Electrical Technician
description:
Role and Responsibilities:

Instrumentation and Signal Processing:

  • Assemble and integrate electronic components and systems according to technical drawings and specifications.
  • Work with various sensors and instrumentation to ensure accurate data collection and processing.
  • Perform tuning tasks to ensure optimal performance of electronic systems.

Testing and Troubleshooting:

  • Conduct functional testing and calibration of electronic components and systems to ensure proper operation.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve issues with sensors, signal processing circuits, and high-temperature devices.
  • Maintain accurate records of test results, troubleshooting processes, and any corrective actions taken.

Quality Assurance:

  • Perform regular inspections of electronic components and assemblies to ensure they meet quality standards.
  • Identify and report any quality issues or defects.
  • Assist in the implementation of quality control procedures and processes.

Documentation and Reporting:

  • Maintain detailed and accurate records of assembly processes, test results, and quality inspections.
  • Prepare and submit reports on production and quality metrics as required.
  • Ensure all documentation complies with company policies and regulatory requirements.

Maintenance and Repair:

  • Perform routine maintenance on electronic tools and equipment to ensure they are in good working condition.
  • Assist in the repair of faulty or damaged electronic components and systems.
  • Ensure a clean and organized work environment, following all safety protocols.

Collaboration and Communication:

  • Work closely with team members to ensure smooth workflow and timely completion of assembly tasks.
  • Communicate effectively with supervisors and team members regarding any issues or improvements.
  • Participate in team meetings and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives.

Skills and Experiences:

  • Strong knowledge of instrumentation and signal processing techniques.
  • Experience with assembling and troubleshooting sensors and high-temperature electronic devices.
  • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
  • Proficiency in using hand and power tools for electronic assembly.

General:

  • Committed to a high standard of safety and willing to comply with all safety laws and all the company’s safety policies and rules. Must be willing to report safety violations and potential safety violations to appropriate supervisory or management personnel.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.

Qualifications and Education Requirements:

  • Diploma in Electronics Engineering, Instrumentation, or a related field.
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in electronics assembly or a similar role.
  • Familiarity with industry standards and regulations for electronic assembly and testing.
  • Certification in relevant technical fields is a plus.

postedCompany.description:
At GOWell, we are continually evolving to meet the growing needs of our customers in the oil and gas, geothermal, and carbon capture industries. Since our founding in 2005, we have expanded globally to better support our customer base. Today, our operations include research and development centers in Houston (USA) and Xian (China), as well as production facilities in Houston, TX (USA), Dubai (UAE), and Xian (China). To further enhance our services, we also maintain technical support centers worldwide, providing comprehensive training on our technologies.

Our journey has been remarkable, and we remain committed to continuous growth while nurturing a warm, collaborative, and family-like environment. At GOWell, every individual makes a difference.
postedCompany.name: GOWELL TECHNOLOGY SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
